Dear Sir or Madam,

I am writing to enquire the safety details of your product (3M 334 Splash Safety Goggle). Since I work in the building industry, my colleagues and I use 334 on a daily basis. There is so far no eye injury thanks to your excellent product quality. Unfortunately, there was a recent social incident in Hong Kong which dented my confidence towards your products.

On 11th August, 2019, a Hong Kong protester suffered from irreversible eye injury caused by high velocity foreign body, despite the fact that she wore the 334 goggle. The foreign body was probably a bean bag round as there were photos showing the round stuck in the broken blood-stained goggle. It is understandable that the safety goggle should not be able to withstand a bean bag round.

Yesterday, on 16th August, 2019, the former Secretary of Food and Health of Hong Kong, Mr Ko Wing-man, made a statement regarding the above tragedy. He stated that the injury was caused by marbles/beads shot by unknown personnel. Such fact astonished me, since 334 meets the Z87.1-2015 safety standard and it is likely to be able to withstand the impact of marbles/beads. If 334, which meets the Z87+ standard, cannot withstand the impact of a projectile coming from a slingshot, can it really safeguard the occupational safety of all users of the goggle? Is our eyesight at immense risk when we wear 334 in our construction sites?

I would like to ask for more information about the safety of your product. Would you mind providing us the most up-to-date test reports regarding whether the 334 goggles can meet the Z87+ standards? If possible, can you provide the full set test report? I would be grateful if you can do so, since it can rebuild our confidence towards your products. Thank you very much and I am looking forward to you reply.

Yours faithfully,
???
